title: analytics — subscribed events
description: Subscribes to own AnalyticsEvent* events for the fan-out pipeline + cross-module lifecycle events (TenantErased, UserErased, IdentityMerged) for compliance-driven cascades. Also subscribes to compliance::ConsentWithdrawn to suppress pending events.

listeners.purpose: Dispatches DispatchAnalyticsEventJob when eligible_provider_count > 0. Skips otherwise (event already terminal).
Dispatches MergeAliasedEventsJob to rewrite subject_id on prior events from AnalyticsIdentity to User.
Same as above but triggered by alias() rather than identify().
Atomic increment of AnalyticsEvent.delivered_provider_count. On reaching eligible_provider_count, transitions status → delivered + fires AnalyticsEventDelivered.
Records success into AnalyticsProviderConfig.circuit_breaker_state. Half-open probe transitions to closed after `close_after_successes` successes.
Records failure into circuit_breaker_state. Threshold or failure_rate breach transitions state → open + fires AnalyticsProviderCircuitBreakerOpened.
Clears the Redis cache of active-providers-per-tenant so the next fan-out picks up the change.
Flushes any queued events for this provider before dropping them. Prevents silent data loss on config change.
Cascade delete: analytics_events + analytics_deliveries + analytics_provider_configs + analytics_identities for the erased tenant. Writes a summary row into audit before the hard-delete.

GDPR Art. 17 cascade: for the erased user_id, (a) redact properties.email + properties.phone + any other subject-identifying properties on their events; (b) set AnalyticsIdentity.identified_user_id = null; (c) hash device_fingerprint_hash if not already. Retains behavioral shape for aggregate reporting; strips per-subject identifiers.

When a subject withdraws analytics consent, mark any pending AnalyticsEvent + AnalyticsDelivery rows for that subject as suppressed_by_consent. Prevents post-withdrawal dispatches for events already captured under the prior consent state.
When two Identity rows merge (post-signup 'link accounts'), consolidate their AnalyticsIdentity rows too. Emit AnalyticsIdentityAliased.
